reports-alv-029.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Defensive code patterns to prevent and investigate production incidents.

Used for:
- Stopping common dumps from empty tables, division by zero and dynamic access
- Differentiating background and foreground error reporting paths
- Diagnosing dumps shown in ST22 and writing safe guards in code
- Producing batch-safe log lines using WRITE instead of MESSAGE in background

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- IF lines( lt_data ) = 0 then MESSAGE TYPE S DISPLAY LIKE W and RETURN
- IF lv_total <> 0 before division to avoid COMPUTE_INT_ZERODIVIDE
- FIELD-SYMBOLS TYPE any with ASSIGN COMPONENT plus IS ASSIGNED guard
- IF sy-batch = abap_true write log line, ELSE MESSAGE TYPE E
- ST22 dump entry is starting point for root-cause analysis

Interview keyword: defensive coding for ST22 dump prevention

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you investigate a short dump (ST22) for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST22 mein, main pehle program name aur time se filter karta hoon relevant dump dhoondhne ke liye. Main char key sections check karta hoon: error ki category samjhne ke liye exception type, active source code extract jo exact failed line dikhata hai, active calls section jo complete call stack dikhata hai, aur system variables section jo runtime values dikhata hai. Is information ke saath, main DEV mein reproduce karta hoon — agar dump ek custom program mein hai toh main us line pe breakpoint set kar sakta hoon aur root cause samjhne ke liye step through kar sakta hoon. Reports ke common dump types: invalid SQL syntax ke liye DBIF_RSQL_INVALID_RSQL, unassigned field symbol access ke liye GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED, aur unhandled class exceptions ke liye RAISE_EXCEPTION.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In ST22, I first filter by program name and time to find the relevant dump. I check four key sections: the exception type to understand the category of error, the active source code extract showing the exact line that failed, the active calls section showing the complete call stack, and the system variables section showing runtime values. With this information, I reproduce in DEV — if the dump is in a custom program I can set a breakpoint at that line and step through to understand the root cause. Common dump types for reports: DBIF_RSQL_INVALID_RSQL for invalid SQL syntax, GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED for unassigned field symbol access, and RAISE_EXCEPTION for unhandled class exceptions.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production dump incident: GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED in ALV report. ST22 showed: line 145, ASSIGN COMPONENT... FIELD-SYMBOL used after assignment check failed. Root cause: dynamic field name changed in a transport — field 'AMOUNT_EUR' renamed to 'AMT_EUR' in structure. Fix: update dynamic field name reference. Transport in 2 hours.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Fixing the dump based only on the exception type without reading the source code extract and active calls. The exception type tells you the category but not the root cause — you need the exact line and call stack for accurate diagnosis.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST22 dump prevention patterns
" GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED prevention:
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <val> TYPE any.
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'NETWR' OF STRUCTURE ls_row TO <val>.
IF <val> IS ASSIGNED.     " Always check before use
  DATA(lv_amount) = <val>.
ENDIF.

" DBIF_RSQL prevention — validate input before SELECT
IF p_bukrs IS INITIAL.
  MESSAGE 'Company code required' TYPE 'E'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.
" Now safe to use p_bukrs in WHERE clause
SELECT * FROM bsik WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs INTO TABLE @lt_bsik.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you debug wrong data output in a production ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Wrong data ke liye mera approach: pehle user se ek specific example lo — document number, material ya order number. Expected value ko SE16 ya source tables mein manually verify karo. Phir report jo dikhata hai usse compare karke exact discrepancy samjho. ABAP debugger mein, main wrong value wale field pe ek watchpoint set karta hoon aur specific input ke saath run karta hoon — debugger tab stop hota hai jab value wrong wali pe change hoti hai, exactly konsi line ne cause kiya yeh dikhate hue. Common root causes: wrong JOIN condition duplicates produce karte hue jo totals inflate karte hain, missing filter extra rows cause karte hue, currency properly converted nahi, ya date logic off by one.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: My approach for wrong data: first get a specific example from the user — document number, material, or order number. Manually verify the expected value in SE16 or the source tables. Then compare with what the report shows to understand the exact discrepancy. In the ABAP debugger, I set a watchpoint on the field that has the wrong value and run with the specific input — the debugger stops when the value changes to the wrong one, showing exactly which line caused it. Common root causes: wrong JOIN condition producing duplicates that inflate totals, missing filter causing extra rows, currency not converted properly, or date logic off by one.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Wrong data incident: report showing doubled amounts for some orders. Root cause: LEFT JOIN with VBAP without unique key — one VBAK row joining two VBAP rows with same condition, doubling the header net value. Fix: aggregate in SELECT or use DISTINCT. Lesson: always verify JOIN conditions produce expected row count.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming the database data is wrong when the report shows wrong values — always verify source tables first with SE16 before blaming the code. Often the data is correct and the report logic has a bug.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Debug wrong totals — check for duplicate joins
" WRONG: Can produce duplicate header rows if join is 1-to-many
SELECT a~vbeln, a~netwr, b~posnr
  FROM vbak AS a
  JOIN vbap AS b ON b~vbeln = a~vbeln  " One header, many items!
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_wrong).           " NETWR repeated per item

" CORRECT: Aggregate or use header-only SELECT
SELECT vbeln, SUM( netwr ) AS netwr
  FROM vbak
  WHERE vkorg = @p_vkorg
  GROUP BY vbeln
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_correct).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a background job failure in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek background job fail hota hai, main yeh process follow karta hoon: SM37 check karta hoon job status ke liye aur exact error message ke liye job log open karta hoon. Agar yeh ek program dump hai, main dump details ke liye ST22 check karta hoon. Agar yeh batch user ke liye ek authorization failure hai, main batch user ke context mein SU53 run karta hoon. Agar variant mein invalid selection criteria hai jo no data deti hai, main variant update karta hoon. DEV mein root cause fix karne aur QA mein test karne ke baad, main fix production pe apply karta hoon. Immediate run ke liye, main ya toh SM36 mein job restart karta hoon ya manually trigger karta hoon agar window abhi bhi valid hai. Main hamesha stakeholders ko delay aur expected resolution time inform karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a background job fails, I follow this process: check SM37 for the job status and open the job log for the exact error message. If it is a program dump, I check ST22 for the dump details. If it is an authorization failure for the batch user, I run SU53 in context of the batch user. If the variant has invalid selection criteria giving no data, I update the variant. After fixing the root cause in DEV and testing in QA, I apply the fix to production. For the immediate run, I either restart the job in SM36 or trigger it manually if the window is still valid. I always inform stakeholders of the delay and expected resolution time.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Monday morning incident: month-end report not in spool. SM37: job finished but spool empty. Job log: 'No data found.' Root cause: variant had fiscal period 12 of previous year hardcoded — not updated for new year. Immediate fix: update variant to new fiscal year. Restart job manually. Report in spool within 10 minutes. Permanent fix: use dynamic fiscal period calculation in ABAP instead of hardcoded value.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Immediately rerunning a failed job without diagnosing the root cause — if the cause is a program dump, rerunning will just fail again. Always diagnose first, fix, then rerun.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Prevent hardcoded period in background reports
" WRONG: Hardcoded period in variant
" p_gjahr = '2024'  p_monat = '012'  " Will fail next year

" CORRECT: Dynamic current period calculation
DATA: lv_gjahr TYPE gjahr,
      lv_monat TYPE monat.
" Get current fiscal period using FI function
CALL FUNCTION 'FI_PERIOD_DETERMINE'
  EXPORTING  i_budat = sy-datum  i_bukrs = p_bukrs
  IMPORTING  e_gjahr = lv_gjahr  e_monat = lv_monat
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 1.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  lv_gjahr = sy-datum(4).  " Fallback: calendar year
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What do you do when an ALV report is extremely slow in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production mein ek critically slow report ke liye, meri immediate action hai isse ek background job ke roop mein schedule karna user productivity restore karne ke liye jab tak main investigate karta hoon — yeh users ko waiting se prevent karta hai. Diagnosis ke liye, main production mein ST05 SQL trace read-only mode mein run karta hoon code changes ke bina queries capture karne ke liye. Main slowest statements ke liye trace analyze karta hoon. Common findings: BASIS involvement require karne wala missing database index, SELECT inside LOOP, proper WHERE conditions ke bina SELECT. Main DEV mein fix develop karta hoon, QA mein production-volume data ke saath test karta hoon, emergency change approval leta hoon aur deploy karta hoon. Main before-after performance metrics document karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For a critically slow report in production, my immediate action is to schedule it as a background job to restore user productivity while I investigate — this prevents users from waiting. For diagnosis, I run ST05 SQL trace in production in read-only mode to capture the queries without code changes. I analyze the trace for the slowest statements. Common findings: missing database index requiring BASIS involvement, SELECT inside LOOP, SELECT without proper WHERE conditions. I develop the fix in DEV, test with production-volume data in QA, get emergency change approval, and deploy. I document the before-after performance metrics.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production performance ticket SLA: resolve within 4 hours. Report taking 12 minutes. Immediate action: convert to background job for user — 5 minutes. Diagnosis: 45 minutes (ST05 trace). Root cause: MAKT table join done in ABAP loop — 30,000 iterations. Fix: FOR ALL ENTRIES. DEV fix: 30 minutes. QA test: 20 minutes. Emergency transport: 30 minutes. Total: 2.5 hours. Result: 12 minutes → 40 seconds.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Making performance fixes in production directly — even for urgent issues, always fix in DEV first and transport. Production direct changes cause transport inconsistencies and are not traceable.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Temporary immediate relief — background scheduling from ABAP
" If user runs report and it is too slow, redirect to background
IF sy-batch = abap_false AND lv_data_count > 10000.
  DATA lv_answer TYPE c.
  CALL FUNCTION 'POPUP_TO_CONFIRM'
    EXPORTING  titlebar  = 'Large Data Volume'
               text_question = |{ lv_data_count } records found. Run in background?|
    IMPORTING  answer    = lv_answer.
  IF lv_answer = '1'.  " User confirmed background
    SUBMIT (sy-repid) WITH SELECTION-TABLE lt_selparams
      VIA JOB 'ZRPT_BACKGROUND' NUMBER lv_jobnum
      AND RETURN.
    MESSAGE 'Job submitted — check SM37 for results' TYPE 'S'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you resolve an authorization-related data issue in an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization-related data issues ke liye, main ek two-track investigation follow karta hoon. Pehle, main affected user ko issue ke immediately baad SU53 run karne ke liye kehta hoon — yeh exact object, activity aur value ke saath last failed authorization check dikhata hai. Doosra, main ABAP code review karta hoon AUTHORITY-CHECK statements aur kisi bhi dynamic WHERE conditions ke liye jo user authorizations ke based pe data filter karte hain. Kabhi-kabhi issue ek missing role nahi hota balki ek WHERE condition hoti hai jo ek field se filter karti hai jis tak user ka access nahi hai. Fix ya toh Security team ke saath missing authorization assign karne ke liye coordinate karna hai, ya code logic correct karna hai agar AUTHORITY-CHECK ke wrong field values hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For authorization-related data issues, I follow a two-track investigation. First, I ask the affected user to run SU53 immediately after the issue — it shows the last failed authorization check with the exact object, activity, and value. Second, I review the ABAP code for AUTHORITY-CHECK statements and any dynamic WHERE conditions that filter data based on user authorizations. Sometimes the issue is not a missing role but a WHERE condition that filters by a field the user does not have access to. The fix is either coordinating with the Security team to assign the missing authorization, or correcting the code logic if the AUTHORITY-CHECK has wrong field values.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Incident: user seeing no purchase orders in PO report. SU53: no failed check — not an auth object issue. Code debug: WHERE clause had EKKO-EKORG = p_ekorg but user had only entered one org code and their POs were in a different org. Not an authorization bug — user error. Resolution: better selection help on the EKORG field with user's authorized values. SU53 ruled out auth issue in 5 minutes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Raising a Basis ticket immediately for authorization issues without first debugging the ABAP code — often the problem is in code logic, not the role assignment. Check SU53 and code both before involving Basis.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Auth check with proper error message
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_BEST_BSA'
  ID 'BSART' FIELD p_bsart
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  " Clear message — user knows what to do
  MESSAGE e001(zmsgs) WITH p_bsart
    'Not authorized for PO document type. Contact system admin.'.
ENDIF.
" Also: show only authorized orgs in F4 help
SELECT DISTINCT ekorg FROM t024e
  WHERE mandt = sy-mandt
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orgs).
" Filter by authorization in F4 values

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle an emergency hotfix transport for a production ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Emergency hotfixes ke liye, main ek fast-track lekin controlled process follow karta hoon: koi bhi code touch karne se pehle main change control manager se verbal ya written approval leta hoon. Main DEV mein minimum targeted change karta hoon — sirf affected code touch karte hue, unrelated sections ko refactor ya clean up nahi. Main fix ko ongoing Sprint changes se isolate karne ke liye ek separate emergency transport request create karta hoon. QA mein ek quick smoke test ke baad — specific fix aur key regression scenarios pe focused — main transport ko approval documentation ke saath BASIS ko hand karta hoon. Post-deployment, main production mein verify karta hoon, root cause analysis ke saath incident ticket update karta hoon aur preventive measures document karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For emergency hotfixes, I follow a fast-track but controlled process: I get verbal or written approval from the change control manager before touching any code. I make the minimum targeted change in DEV — touching only the affected code, not refactoring or cleaning up unrelated sections. I create a separate emergency transport request to isolate the fix from ongoing Sprint changes. After a quick smoke test in QA — focused on the specific fix and key regression scenarios — I hand the transport to BASIS with the approval documentation. Post-deployment, I verify in production, update the incident ticket with root cause analysis, and document preventive measures.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Emergency: financial report showing negative amounts in closing report — month-end in progress. Root cause: sign reversal bug — credit memo amounts not negated. Code fix: 3 lines. Separate hotfix transport created. QA test: 20 minutes. Manager approval email. BASIS import to PROD: 15 minutes. Total elapsed: 55 minutes. Report verified, month-end closed successfully.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Including unrelated code cleanup or enhancements in an emergency hotfix transport — increases risk of introducing new issues. Emergency fix should be minimal and surgical — exactly the lines needed to fix the reported issue, nothing more.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Emergency fix documentation comment — always add to hotfix
"--------------------------------------------------------------
" HOTFIX: INC-2024-0847
" Date: 2024-01-15  Developer: John Dev  Approved: PM Name
" Issue: Credit memo amounts shown as positive in aging report
" Fix: Negate DMBTR for credit memo document types
"--------------------------------------------------------------
LOOP AT lt_aging ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<row>).
  IF <row>-blart = 'KG' OR <row>-blart = 'RE'.  " Credit memo types
    <row>-dmbtr = <row>-dmbtr * -1.  " HOTFIX: negate for display
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate during a production incident for reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek production incident ke dauran, main ek communication cadence follow karta hoon: report receive karne ke 5 se 10 minutes ke andar immediate acknowledgment, followed by regular status updates har 30 se 45 minutes — even bina resolution ke — stakeholders ko jaanna chahiye ki koi actively work kar raha hai. Main business terms mein communicate karta hoon, technical jargon mein nahi — 'SELECT inside LOOP causing N+1 query problem' nahi balki 'report bahut zyada individual database calls kar raha tha; humne bottleneck identify aur fix kar diya hai.' Main realistic ETAs set karta hoon aur agar woh change hote hain, main proactively communicate karta hoon. Post-resolution, main ek brief root cause summary bhejta hoon aur recurrence prevent karne ke liye hum konse steps le rahe hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: During a production incident, I follow a communication cadence: immediate acknowledgment within 5 to 10 minutes of receiving the report, followed by regular status updates every 30 to 45 minutes even without a resolution — stakeholders need to know someone is actively working on it. I communicate in business terms, not technical jargon — not 'SELECT inside LOOP causing N+1 query problem' but 'the report was making too many individual database calls; we have identified and fixed the bottleneck.' I set realistic ETAs and if they change, I proactively communicate. Post-resolution, I send a brief root cause summary and what steps we are taking to prevent recurrence.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Incident communication example: '09:15 - Issue acknowledged, investigating. 09:45 - Root cause identified: background job variant has expired fiscal year. Fixing now. 10:00 - Fix applied, job restarted. Report will be in spool by 10:15. 10:20 - Confirmed: report in spool, verified with finance team. Full RCA will be shared by EOD.' — Clear, timely, business-language updates kept stakeholders calm.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Going silent during an incident — stakeholders escalate when they do not hear updates. Even a message saying 'still investigating, no ETA yet' is better than silence. Set a communication timer for every 30-45 minutes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" No code for communication — but ABAP for RCA prevention
" Post-incident prevention: add monitoring check
REPORT zrpt_variant_check.  " New monitoring report
DATA lt_jobs TYPE TABLE OF tbtcjob.
SELECT jobname, variant FROM tbtcjob
  WHERE jobclass = 'A'  " Class A = high priority
    AND status   = 'S'  " S = Scheduled
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_scheduled).
LOOP AT lt_scheduled INTO DATA(ls_job).
  " Check if variant still valid
  CALL FUNCTION 'RS_VARIANT_EXISTS'
    EXPORTING log_report = ls_job-jobname variant = ls_job-variant
    EXCEPTIONS not_found = 1.
  IF sy-subrc = 1.
    WRITE: / 'WARNING: Variant missing for job:', ls_job-jobname.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: What monitoring setup do you recommend for production ALV reports?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production ALV monitoring setup: failure pe email alerts ke saath background job tracking ke liye SM37. Spool monitoring ke liye SP01 — ensure karna ki output correctly generate ho raha hai. Program name se filtered dump monitoring ke liye ST22. Stuck long-running reports identify karne ke liye SM50. Performance ke liye: degradation early catch karne ke liye heavy reports pe ST05 trace quarterly. Scheduled reports ko affect karne wali maintenance windows ke baare mein business users ko proactively notify karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Production ALV monitoring setup: SM37 for background job tracking with email alerts on failure. SP01 for spool monitoring — ensure output generating correctly. ST22 for dump monitoring filtered by program name. SM50 for identifying stuck long-running reports. For performance: ST05 trace quarterly on heavy reports to catch degradation early. Proactively notify business users about maintenance windows affecting scheduled reports.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Set up proactive monitoring: SM37 email alert for critical month-end report. Added ABAP code to email row count and execution time to business users after each run — they immediately detect incomplete data. Support tickets reduced 70% after this monitoring was implemented.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Waiting for users to report ALV issues instead of proactive monitoring. By the time user calls, business impact already happened. SM37 + ST22 proactive monitoring catches issues before users notice.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Add execution summary to ALV
DATA: lv_count TYPE i,
      lv_start TYPE timestampl,
      lv_end   TYPE timestampl.
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_start.
* ... ALV display logic ...
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_end.
lv_count = lines( lt_output ).
* Log: lv_count rows, time = lv_end - lv_start seconds
WRITE: / lv_count, 'rows in', lv_end - lv_start, 'seconds'.

reports-alv-030.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: interviewer jab poochhe 'Real-time ticket solve kiya hai Reports/ALV related?' — toh wo test kar raha hai ki tum production pressure mein kaam kar sakte ho ya nahi. Ye ek hidden question hai — actually wo poochh raha hai: 'Kya tum sirf theory jaante ho ya actual problem-solving experience hai?' Best answer: ek specific ticket ka STAR format story batao — Situation (kya hua), Task (tumhara kaam kya tha), Action (tum ne kya kiya step by step), Result (kya fix hua, kitna time laga). Example story types: (1) Column blank — fieldname mismatch in field catalog. (2) Report bahut slow — SELECT inside LOOP fix. (3) Background job fail — variant issue. (4) Amount doubled — duplicate JOIN. (5) Authorization — user data nahi dikh raha. Ye sab real tickets hain jo har project mein aate hain. Agar tum fresher ho toh training project ya certification lab ka example do — lekin honestly bolo 'training project mein yeh scenario solve kiya.' Experienced developer ke liye: 1-2 specific production examples, real numbers, real tools used. Confidence se bolna ki 'maine ye kiya, ye result aya' — ye impress karta hai.
reports-alv-030.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Ticket stories ke liye 5 best types yaad rakh lo — ye interview mein most effective hain:

1. Performance Ticket (Most Common):
   Story: 'Vendor aging report QA mein 8 minutes le rahi thi. ST05 trace chalaaya. 50,000 vendor name fetches LOOP ke andar — 50,000 individual SELECT calls to LFA1. FOR ALL ENTRIES se fix kiya. 8 minutes se 42 seconds. Client approved, transported to production.'

2. Wrong Data Ticket (Shows Debugging Skill):
   Story: 'Finance team ne bola GL account balance report mein amounts wrong hain — doubled dikhte hain for some accounts. Debug kiya. Root cause: BSEG JOIN with BKPF without unique key producing duplicate rows — accounts with multiple document types doubled. Fix: add document type filter or use DISTINCT. Verified with SE16 data match.'

3. Background Job Ticket (Shows Production Support):
   Story: 'Monday morning 9 AM — finance manager escalated: monthly report nahi aaya spool mein. SM37: job status Finished but spool empty. Job log: No records found. Root cause: variant mein previous year fiscal period hardcoded tha — new year mein data nahi mila. Variant update kiya, job manually restart kiya, spool 10 minutes mein aaya.'

4. Column Blank Ticket (Shows Detail Attention):
   Story: 'User ne bola report mein Net Amount column blank hai. Report run, no dump, data fetch correct. Field catalog check kiya — fieldname written as NET_VALUE but internal table field was NETWR. Single character fix. Lesson: always copy fieldname from TYPE definition, never type manually.'

5. Authorization Ticket (Shows Security Awareness):
   Story: 'User X bola Company Code 1200 ka data nahi dikh raha but User Y ko dikh raha hai. SU53 chalaaya — missing authorization for F_BKPF_BUK with activity 03 for bukrs 1200. Basis team ko auth object assignment diya. Code side: AUTHORITY-CHECK already correct — just missing role assignment.'

Each story should have: numbers (time saved, rows, error code), tools used (ST05, ST22, SM37, SU53, debugger), and outcome (fixed in X time, transported, user confirmed).
reports-alv-030.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Yes, I have resolved multiple production tickets related to reports and ALV. One specific ticket I remember clearly was a performance issue on a Sales Register report. The report was taking 11 minutes to run for a one-month date range with around 30,000 orders. I ran ST05 SQL trace and found that inside a LOOP over the orders, there was a SELECT statement fetching customer names from KNA1 — resulting in 30,000 individual database calls. I replaced this with a single FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT after the initial data fetch. After the fix, the same report ran in under 50 seconds. I tested in QA with production-equivalent data, got approvals, and transported to production. The finance team confirmed the improvement the next morning.'

Hinglish tip: Specific numbers zaroor use karo — '11 minutes', '30,000 orders', '50 seconds'. Tools naam lo — ST05, FOR ALL ENTRIES. End with user confirmation. Ye story format interview mein sabse zyada impact deta hai. 2-3 minute answer aim karo — not too short, not rambling.
reports-alv-030.codeExamples:
" Real ticket fix example — Performance: SELECT inside LOOP
" BEFORE (caused 11 minute runtime — production ticket)
LOOP AT lt_vbak INTO DATA(ls_order).
  SELECT SINGLE name1 FROM kna1    " 30,000 individual calls!
    WHERE kunnr = @ls_order-kunnr
    INTO @ls_order-name1.
  MODIFY lt_vbak FROM ls_order.
ENDLOOP.

" AFTER FIX (50 seconds — same output)
IF lt_vbak IS NOT INITIAL.
  SELECT kunnr, name1 FROM kna1
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_vbak
    WHERE kunnr = @lt_vbak-kunnr
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_kna1).
ENDIF.
" Use SORT + READ TABLE for fast lookup
SORT lt_kna1 BY kunnr.
LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ord>).
  READ TABLE lt_kna1 INTO DATA(ls_cust)
    WITH KEY kunnr = <ord>-kunnr BINARY SEARCH.
  IF sy-subrc = 0. <ord>-name1 = ls_cust-name1. ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

I replaced this with a single FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT after the initial data fetch. After the fix, the same report ran in under 50 seconds. I tested in QA with production-equivalent data, got approvals, and transported to production. The finance team confirmed the improvement the next morning.

Ek specific ticket jo mujhe clearly yaad hai woh Sales Register report pe performance issue tha. Report 11 minutes le raha tha ek month date range ke liye around 30,000 orders ke saath. commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting to check FOR ALL ENTRIES empty table condition — if lt_vbak were empty and FOR ALL ENTRIES was used, all KNA1 rows would be fetched.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always add IF lt_vbak IS NOT INITIAL before FOR ALL ENTRIES.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: When amounts are doubled, first assumption is often authorization or data entry error — but JOIN duplication is more common cause.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always check the row count first: if SELECT COUNT of JOIN is double the expected rows, the JOIN condition is the issue.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Restarting the job without updating the variant — it would fail again with the same message.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always fix the root cause (variant or code) before restarting. Also: not informing stakeholders of the delay causes unnecessary escalation.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Spending time looking at data fetch logic when the column is blank — the first check for a blank column should always be the field catalog fieldname match.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Single-row read — dangerous inside LOOP, causes N+1 pattern
Batch read replacement that drops SELECT-in-LOOP runtime drastically
Updates the row in place — used in the slow before-fix version
LOOP with field symbol to avoid work-area copy overhead
Required before BINARY SEARCH for fast READ TABLE lookups
O(log n) lookup after SORT — used to fetch name1 by kunnr
Customer master general data used for customer name lookup
Sales document header table iterated in the loop
SQL trace Tcode used to confirm the N+1 pattern before fix
Guard required before FOR ALL ENTRIES to avoid full-table read

reports-alv-030.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Real ticket where SELECT inside LOOP was cut from 11 minutes to 50 seconds.

Used for:
- Replacing the N+1 SELECT pattern with FOR ALL ENTRIES plus SORT plus BINARY SEARCH
- Demonstrating ST05-driven root-cause analysis on a production performance ticket
- Showing field-symbol LOOP to avoid work-area copy overhead
- Talking through the before and after numbers credibly in an interview

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- BEFORE: LOOP AT lt_vbak with SELECT SINGLE name1 FROM KNA1 per row
- AFTER: IF lt_vbak IS NOT INITIAL then SELECT FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_vbak on KNA1
- SORT lt_kna1 BY kunnr then READ TABLE WITH KEY BINARY SEARCH
- LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ord>) for direct update
- ST05 trace confirms reduced database hits and total time

Interview keyword: FAE plus SORT plus BINARY SEARCH fix

followupAnswerBank.question: Tell me about a specific performance ticket you resolved for an ALV report.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek production support scenario mein, mujhe ek ticket mila ki ek Sales Register report ek one-month date range ke liye 11 minutes le rahi thi. Maine ST05 SQL trace run kiya aur identify kiya ki 30,000 order rows mein se har ek ke liye, program customer name fetch karne ke liye KNA1 ke against ek SELECT SINGLE execute kar raha tha — jisse 30,000 database calls result mein aaye. Maine is pattern ko main data fetch ke baad ek FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT se replace kiya, ek SORT aur lookup ke liye binary SEARCH READ ke saath combined. Execution time 11 minutes se 50 seconds tak gir gaya. Maine QA mein same data volume ke saath test kiya, change document kiya aur production pe transport kiya.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In a production support scenario, I received a ticket that a Sales Register report was taking 11 minutes for a one-month date range. I ran ST05 SQL trace and identified that for each of the 30,000 order rows, the program was executing a SELECT SINGLE against KNA1 to fetch the customer name — resulting in 30,000 database calls. I replaced this pattern with a FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT after the main data fetch, combined with a SORT and binary SEARCH READ for lookup. The execution time dropped from 11 minutes to 50 seconds. I tested with the same data volume in QA, documented the change, and transported to production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-fix lesson: added this check to my personal code review checklist — 'scan for SELECT inside LOOP before every code delivery.' Also added to team's peer review guidelines. Two sprints later: another developer's report flagged in review for same pattern — prevented before it reached production.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Forgetting to check FOR ALL ENTRIES empty table condition — if lt_vbak were empty and FOR ALL ENTRIES was used, all KNA1 rows would be fetched. Always add IF lt_vbak IS NOT INITIAL before FOR ALL ENTRIES.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" The fix — documented for ticket resolution
" Ticket: INC-2024-1234 | Category: Performance | Priority: High
" Root Cause: SELECT inside LOOP — 30k database calls
" Fix: FOR ALL ENTRIES + Binary Search
IF lt_vbak IS NOT INITIAL.  " Critical guard condition
  SELECT kunnr, name1 FROM kna1
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @lt_vbak
    WHERE kunnr = @lt_vbak-kunnr
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cust).
  SORT lt_cust BY kunnr.  " Enable binary search
ENDIF.
" Lookup in loop
READ TABLE lt_cust WITH KEY kunnr = ls_order-kunnr
  BINARY SEARCH INTO DATA(ls_name).

followupAnswerBank.question: Describe a ticket where wrong data was showing in an ALV report.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek finance team ne report kiya ki GL balance report kuch accounts ke liye doubled amounts dikha rahi thi. Maine pehle SE16 mein BSEG ke against expected amounts manually verify kiye — database values correct the. Report ko debug mode mein run karte hue, maine aggregation logic trace ki aur paya ki BKPF aur BSEG ke beech ek JOIN proper key restriction ke bina tha — multiple line items wale documents ke liye, header data repeat ho raha tha, aggregated amount line items ki number se multiplied ho raha tha. Fix yeh tha ki sirf BSEG level pe aggregate karo bina unnecessarily BKPF join kiye, ya aggregate query pe DISTINCT use karo. Fix ke baad, amounts exactly SE16 se match hue.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A finance team reported that the GL balance report was showing doubled amounts for certain accounts. I first verified the expected amounts manually in SE16 against BSEG — the database values were correct. Running the report in debug mode, I traced the aggregation logic and found a JOIN between BKPF and BSEG without a proper key restriction — for documents with multiple line items, the header data was repeating, causing the aggregated amount to be multiplied by the number of line items. The fix was to aggregate at the BSEG level only without joining BKPF unnecessarily, or to use DISTINCT on the aggregate query. After fix, amounts matched SE16 exactly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: After fix: added a verification step in the report itself — a cross-check that total amount from report matches a direct SELECT SUM from BSEG. If mismatch > 0.01, log a warning. This proactive check would catch similar issues earlier in future runs.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: When amounts are doubled, first assumption is often authorization or data entry error — but JOIN duplication is more common cause. Always check the row count first: if SELECT COUNT of JOIN is double the expected rows, the JOIN condition is the issue.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Ticket fix — remove duplicate join causing doubled amounts
" BEFORE (WRONG): BKPF join producing duplicate rows per line item
SELECT a~belnr, a~bukrs, b~dmbtr
  FROM bkpf AS a
  JOIN bseg AS b ON b~belnr = a~belnr  " Many items per header!
    AND b~bukrs = a~bukrs
  WHERE a~bukrs = @p_bukrs
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_wrong).

" AFTER FIX: Fetch from BSEG directly with specific item fields
SELECT belnr, bukrs, hkont, SUM( dmbtr ) AS dmbtr
  FROM bseg
  WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs AND gjahr = @p_gjahr
  GROUP BY belnr, bukrs, hkont
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_correct).

followupAnswerBank.question: Describe a background job failure ticket you resolved.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek Monday morning, finance manager ne escalate kiya ki monthly vendor payment report spool mein nahi thi. Maine SM37 check kiya — job status Finished show ho raha tha lekin koi spool created nahi tha. Job log ek message dikha raha tha: No data found for the selected period. Maine job dwara used variant examine kiya — usmein previous year ka fiscal period 12 hardcoded tha. New year ka data existed tha lekin variant updated nahi tha. Maine variant ko current fiscal period pe update kiya, job ko manually restart kiya, aur yeh spool mein correct output ke saath successfully complete hua. Permanent fix ke roop mein, maine ABAP program ko modify kiya FI_PERIOD_DETERMINE function module use karke current fiscal period dynamically determine karne ke liye bajaaye ek manually maintained variant value pe rely karne ke.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: On a Monday morning, the finance manager escalated that the monthly vendor payment report was not in the spool. I checked SM37 — job status showed Finished but no spool had been created. The job log showed a message: No data found for the selected period. I examined the variant used by the job — it had fiscal period 12 of the previous year hardcoded. The new year's data existed but the variant was not updated. I updated the variant to the current fiscal period, manually restarted the job, and it completed successfully with the correct output in spool. As a permanent fix, I modified the ABAP program to dynamically determine the current fiscal period using the FI_PERIOD_DETERMINE function module instead of relying on a manually maintained variant value.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-incident action: documented a checklist for month-end job setup — includes variant review at start of each fiscal year. Sent to functional team who owns the variant maintenance. Also: added a validation in the program to warn if the selected period is more than 3 months in the past — helps catch stale variants earlier.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Restarting the job without updating the variant — it would fail again with the same message. Always fix the root cause (variant or code) before restarting. Also: not informing stakeholders of the delay causes unnecessary escalation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Permanent fix — dynamic fiscal period
DATA: lv_gjahr TYPE gjahr,
      lv_poper TYPE poper.
CALL FUNCTION 'FI_PERIOD_DETERMINE'
  EXPORTING i_budat = sy-datum  i_bukrs = p_bukrs
  IMPORTING e_gjahr = lv_gjahr  e_poper = lv_poper
  EXCEPTIONS fiscal_year = 1  period_not_found = 2  OTHERS = 3.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  lv_gjahr = sy-datum(4).  " Fallback: use calendar year
  lv_poper = sy-datum+4(2). " Use calendar month
ENDIF.
" Use dynamic values — no hardcoding
SELECT * FROM bkpf WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs
  AND gjahr = @lv_gjahr AND monat = @lv_poper
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs).

followupAnswerBank.question: Describe a ticket where a column was blank in an ALV report.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek user ne report kiya ki ek procurement report mein Net Amount column completely blank tha — no values, no errors, report otherwise fine work kar rahi thi. Maine ABAP code check kiya aur field catalog mein issue paya: fieldname 'NET_VALUE' free-text string ke roop mein set tha, lekin internal table TYPE definition mein actual field name 'NETWR' tha. ALV silently unrecognized field names ko ignore karta hai aur ek blank column display karta hai bina kisi error message ke. Fieldname string ko exactly 'NETWR' mein change karne se issue immediately resolve ho gaya. Us point se, maine hamesha field names ko directly TYPE ya TYPES definition se copy karne ki practice adopt kar li bajaaye manually type karne ke.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A user reported that the Net Amount column in a procurement report was completely blank — no values, no errors, report otherwise working fine. I checked the ABAP code and found the issue in the field catalog: the fieldname was set to 'NET_VALUE' as a free-text string, but the actual field name in the internal table TYPE definition was 'NETWR'. ALV silently ignores unrecognized field names and displays a blank column without any error message. Changing the fieldname string to exactly 'NETWR' resolved the issue immediately. From that point, I adopted the practice of always copying field names directly from the TYPE or TYPES definition rather than typing them manually.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Root cause analysis shared with team: ALV field catalog fieldname is case-sensitive and must be uppercase and exactly matching the structure field. Added a unit test concept: after building field catalog, loop through it and verify each fieldname exists in the output structure using DESCRIBE TABLE or RTTI — this would catch such mismatches during development.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Spending time looking at data fetch logic when the column is blank — the first check for a blank column should always be the field catalog fieldname match. Data could be correct and only the display mapping is wrong.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Ticket fix — fieldname mismatch
" BEFORE (WRONG): Free-text fieldname with typo
ls_fcat-fieldname = 'NET_VALUE'.  " WRONG — field is NETWR
ls_fcat-coltext   = 'Net Amount'.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.

" AFTER FIX: Copy exact name from TYPE definition
" TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_output,
"   netwr TYPE vbak-netwr,  " <- exact name here
" END OF ty_output.
ls_fcat-fieldname = 'NETWR'.      " Exact match — works correctly
ls_fcat-coltext   = 'Net Amount'.
ls_fcat-cfieldname = 'WAERK'.     " Also add currency reference
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.

followupAnswerBank.question: Tell me about a ticket involving ALV layout variants in production.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek user ne report kiya ki woh ek ALV report mein apna preferred column arrangement save nahi kar paa rahe — toolbar mein Save Layout option greyed out tha. Maine ABAP code check kiya aur paya ki I_SAVE parameter ek space pe set tha, layout save feature ko effectively disable karte hue. Maine yeh bhi notice kiya ki IS_VARIANT-report sy-repid pe set nahi tha. Maine I_SAVE ko 'A' mein change kiya user-specific aur global layouts dono allow karne ke liye, aur IS_VARIANT ko sy-repid se populate kiya. Fix transport karne ke baad, users apne layouts save aur recall karne mein able hue. Ek related issue post-go-live aaya: DEV mein created default layout production pe transport nahi hua tha — mujhe usse directly production mein ALV toolbar ke through recreate karna pada.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A user reported that they could not save their preferred column arrangement in an ALV report — the Save Layout option was greyed out in the toolbar. I checked the ABAP code and found that the I_SAVE parameter was set to a space, effectively disabling the layout save feature. I also noticed IS_VARIANT-report was not set to sy-repid. I changed I_SAVE to 'A' to allow both user-specific and global layouts, and populated IS_VARIANT with sy-repid. After transporting the fix, users were able to save and recall their layouts. A related issue came up post-go-live: the default layout created in DEV was not transported to production — I had to recreate it directly in production via the ALV toolbar.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live checklist updated after this incident: add 'verify layout variant transport or manual recreation in PROD' as a separate checklist item. Variants do not always come with the program transport — they need explicit transport object addition or manual recreation in the target system.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Setting I_SAVE = 'A' but forgetting IS_VARIANT-report = sy-repid. Without the report name in the variant structure, SAP cannot associate the saved layouts with the correct report — save appears to work but layouts do not reload next time.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Ticket fix — enable layout variant save
" BEFORE (WRONG): I_SAVE empty, IS_VARIANT not set
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING
    i_save  = ' '           " WRONG: Save disabled!
  TABLES t_outtab = lt_data.

" AFTER FIX:
DATA ls_variant TYPE disvariant.
ls_variant-report  = sy-repid.    " Must set program name
ls_variant-variant = '/DEFAULT'.  " Optional: default layout name
CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
  EXPORTING
    i_save     = 'A'          " A = Allow all saves (user + global)
    i_default  = 'X'          " Allow setting default layout
    is_variant = ls_variant   " Link layouts to this program
  TABLES t_outtab = lt_data.

followupAnswerBank.question: Describe a ticket where a report produced a dump (short dump) in production.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek production dump ticket aaya users ke saath jo material stock report run karte waqt ek red error screen report kar rahe the. Maine ST22 open kiya, last hour ke andar dump dhoondha, aur details check kiye: program ZSTOCK_REPORT mein line 287 pe exception GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED. Source code extract dikha raha tha ek FIELD-SYMBOL ko IS ASSIGNED check kiye bina dereferenced kiya ja raha tha. Field symbol ek dynamic field name ke saath ASSIGN COMPONENT ke through assigned tha — jab ek different structure wali new material category introduce ki gayi, field component ab exist nahi karta tha aur assignment silently fail ho gaya. Fix: use se pehle IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED check add karo. DEV mein tested, production mein 3 hours ke andar transported.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A production dump ticket came in with users reporting a red error screen when running the material stock report. I opened ST22, found the dump within the last hour, and checked the details: exception GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED at line 287 in program ZSTOCK_REPORT. The source code extract showed a FIELD-SYMBOL being dereferenced without checking IS ASSIGNED. The field symbol was assigned via ASSIGN COMPONENT with a dynamic field name — when a new material category was introduced with a different structure, the field component no longer existed and the assignment failed silently. Fix: add IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED check before use. Tested in DEV, transported to production within 3 hours.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-fix: reviewed all FIELD-SYMBOL usages in the same program. Found 3 more places without IS ASSIGNED check — fixed all proactively in the same transport. Better to fix potential issues in one transport than face multiple incidents.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Fixing only the exact line that dumped without reviewing nearby similar patterns — often the same anti-pattern exists in multiple places in the same program. Proactive review of all similar code in the same program saves future incidents.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Dump fix — GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED prevention
" BEFORE (caused dump)
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <qty> TYPE any.
ASSIGN COMPONENT lv_fieldname OF STRUCTURE ls_stock TO <qty>.
<qty> = 0.  " DUMP if assignment failed — GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED

" AFTER FIX
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <qty2> TYPE any.
ASSIGN COMPONENT lv_fieldname OF STRUCTURE ls_stock TO <qty2>.
IF <qty2> IS ASSIGNED.     " Always check before use
  <qty2> = 0.
ELSE.
  " Log the issue for investigation
  WRITE: / 'WARNING: Field', lv_fieldname, 'not found in structure'.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: Tell me about a ticket involving ALV Excel export producing wrong output.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek finance user ne report kiya ki ek ALV report ko Excel mein export karne ke baad, amount columns numbers ki bajaaye text ke roop mein show ho rahe the — woh Excel mein SUM formulas use nahi kar paa rahe the. Root cause yeh tha ki amount field mein ek EDIT_MASK field catalog mein set tha jo ALV ko isse currency symbols embedded ek string ke roop mein format karne ke liye cause kar raha tha — Excel phir isse text ke roop mein treat karta tha. Fix yeh tha EDIT_MASK remove karna aur properly CFIELDNAME ko currency reference field pe set karna — ALV phir raw numeric value export karta hai, aur Excel isse ek number ke roop mein recognize karta hai. Currency display Excel ke apne formatting se handle hoti hai jab currency column bhi present hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A finance user reported that after exporting an ALV report to Excel, the amount columns showed as text rather than numbers — they could not use SUM formulas in Excel. The root cause was that the amount field had an EDIT_MASK set in the field catalog causing ALV to format it as a string with currency symbols embedded — Excel then treated it as text. The fix was to remove the EDIT_MASK and properly set CFIELDNAME to the currency reference field — ALV then exports the raw numeric value, and Excel recognizes it as a number. Currency display is handled by Excel's own formatting when the currency column is also present.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Additional finding during fix: the user also complained about German umlauts (ä, ö, ü) in vendor names appearing as question marks in Excel. Root cause: SAP was exporting in SAP code page, Excel opened as UTF-8. Solution: used ABAP2XLSX for this report — it handles encoding correctly and provides proper number formatting. Management approved ABAP2XLSX installation by BASIS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using EDIT_MASK for currency formatting in ALV when the intention is to also export to Excel — EDIT_MASK embeds the currency symbol in the value string, making Excel treat it as text. Use CFIELDNAME for proper currency handling.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Fix for Excel numeric export issue
" BEFORE (WRONG): EDIT_MASK causes text in Excel
ls_fcat-fieldname = 'NETWR'.
ls_fcat-edit_mask = '__,___,__9.99-'.  " Embeds format — Excel text!
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.

" AFTER FIX: Remove edit_mask, use cfieldname
ls_fcat-fieldname  = 'NETWR'.
ls_fcat-coltext    = 'Net Value'.
ls_fcat-cfieldname = 'WAERK'.  " Currency reference — proper format
ls_fcat-do_sum     = abap_true.
ls_fcat-no_zero    = abap_false.  " Show zeros
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
" ALV exports raw number — Excel can SUM correctly

followupAnswerBank.question: Describe a ticket where an ALV report showed no data for a specific user.
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek user ne report kiya ki customer open order report mein no data dikh raha hai jabki colleagues data dekh rahe the. Maine ek three-step investigation follow ki. Pehle, maine user ko SU53 run karne ke liye kaha — koi authorization failure nahi dikha. Doosra, maine selection screen values check kiye jo unhone use kiye — unhone apna regional sales organization enter kiya tha lekin report ek global company code field pe filter kar rahi thi jo unke region ko exclude karti thi. Teesra, maine SE16 mein verify kiya ki unke orders existed. Root cause selection screen initialization mein ek hidden default value tha — AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST event ek default company code set kar raha tha jisme unka sales org included nahi tha. Fix: hidden default remove karo, user ko apna khud ka company code select karne do ya sirf authorized values ke saath proper F4 help add karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A user reported seeing no data in the customer open order report while colleagues were seeing data. I followed a three-step investigation. First, I asked the user to run SU53 — no authorization failure was shown. Second, I checked the selection screen values they used — they had entered their regional sales organization but the report was filtering on a global company code field that excluded their region. Third, I verified in SE16 that their orders existed. The root cause was a hidden default value in the selection screen initialization — the AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST event was setting a default company code that did not include their sales org. Fix: remove the hidden default, let user select their own company code or add proper F4 help with authorized values only.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: This ticket taught me: always make selection criteria visible to the user — hidden defaults or programmatic WHERE conditions that silently filter data cause confusing no-data scenarios. Better: show the effective filter criteria in a TOP_OF_PAGE header so users can see exactly what parameters the report ran with.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Immediately assuming authorization issue and involving Basis without first checking if the data actually exists for the user's selection criteria. SU53 plus SE16 data verification should both be done before raising auth tickets.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Prevention: show effective selection in TOP_OF_PAGE
FORM top_of_page.
  DATA lt_hdr TYPE slis_t_listheader.
  DATA ls_hdr TYPE slis_listheader.
  ls_hdr-typ  = 'S'.  " Selection info type
  ls_hdr-key  = 'Company Code:'.
  ls_hdr-info = p_bukrs.
  APPEND ls_hdr TO lt_hdr.
  CLEAR ls_hdr.
  ls_hdr-typ  = 'S'.
  ls_hdr-key  = 'Date Range:'.
  ls_hdr-info = |{ s_bldat-low } - { s_bldat-high }|.
  APPEND ls_hdr TO lt_hdr.
  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE'
    EXPORTING it_list_commentary = lt_hdr.
ENDFORM.
